title: salvia rypara subsp. platystoma
intro: Salvia rypara subsp. platystoma, also known as Mexican sage, is a subspecies of Salvia rypara, a perennial herb native to Mexico. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, due to the presence of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Salvia rypara subsp. platystoma include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and supporting digestive health. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
